title: Rank-normalized ensemble blending
summary: ROC AUC depends on ordering, not probability calibration.
rationale: ROC AUC depends on ordering, not probability calibration. Public Kaggle ensembling practice often benefits from averaging diverse model ranks, while calibration/logit blending can still help probability shape after rank diversity is established.
implementation_hint: Train several diverse tabular variants: current AutoGluon GBM/XGB, CatBoost with categorical columns, LightGBM with OOF encodings, and a shallow regularized logistic or ExtraTrees model. Blend validation/test predictions by percentile rank or logit average, tune only 3-5 blend weights on OOF AUC, then optionally apply isotonic/Platt calibration without changing rank-heavy behavior.
expected_effect: May add small but meaningful AUC gains by combining models that order edge cases differently around pit windows.
risk: Without proper OOF predictions, blend tuning overfits quickly; rank averaging can also hurt if all models share the same leakage or validation bias.
